best_edges.evidence: pullback_long is the largest real edge: 32 trades, $2530.4 PnL, 259.1 PnL/exposure hour, 100% win rate, no slow >=12h trades. It does carry -403.2 worst MAE, but prior pullback tightening materially improved run_012 to run_015 while over-broad pruning risks starving the core PnL source.

Fast winners account for 58 trades and $4606.3 with median 5.2 minute hold and median MAE -34.5. Many have weak-looking structure/veto fingerprints, so broad structure-score, ADX-slope, or veto-point filters would block too much edge.

breakout_long_1dte contributes 20 trades and $1310.2, about 19% of total PnL, with 169.4 PnL/exposure hour and no slow trades. Two prior quality-tighten attempts reduced score and net profit while worsening drawdown/tail metrics.

charm_pin_pullback_long contributes 37 trades and $1379.5 with a 5.1 minute median hold and average MAE better than pullback_long, but owns the global -527 tail. The edge is real, but it should be split or gated only in the high-extension/delayed-profit state.

squeeze_long has 11 trades, $755 PnL, 186.3 PnL/exposure hour, and worst MAE -208.25. It is smaller than pullback/charm-pin but cleaner than the major tail profiles, so preserve it unless a new-regime test explicitly studies strike-map or trend-day overlap.

broken_edges.evidence: 1DTE local quality gates cut $317-$558 PnL, reduced trades, and worsened worst MAE/drawdown. The 1DTE edge is profitable insurance when 0DTE is unavailable, not a current pruning target.
broken_edges.problem: Prior 1DTE quality tightening failed despite plausible MAE logic.

broken_edges.evidence: Call-wall expansion attempts reduced score by $314-$530 equivalent, cut $305-$482 net profit, and worsened worst MAE to as low as -638.75. Current run has zero call_wall_acceptance_long trades despite enabled profile, so the concept is under-triggered and prior loosening was harmful.
broken_edges.problem: Call-wall acceptance is under-tested and prior sample-builder experiments degraded objective.

broken_edges.action: do not optimize until more sample
broken_edges.evidence: charm_wall_breakaway_long has only 2 trades in latest run. A structure-gate test from run_013 to run_016 lost $178.9 and score while leaving worst MAE unchanged. The profile may have high MFE, but current sample is too thin for stricter config conclusions.
broken_edges.problem: Small-sample charm-wall edits are unstable and recently hurt score.

broken_edges.evidence: STALE/EXPIRED fallback states are only 6 trades and $370.2. Prior fallback pruning was flat-to-negative on score and did not improve worst MAE. Stale breakout fallback is inefficient, but sample is too small for mainline optimization.
broken_edges.problem: No-0DTE fallback evidence is too sparse; pruning does not solve global tail.

broken_edges.action: avoid activation without dedicated discovery run
broken_edges.evidence: breakout_long, wall_reclaim_long, wall_breakout_retest_long, late_day_pin_fade, negative_gamma_momentum_long, and trend_day_pullback_long have zero trades while disabled. They may be valid dormant concepts, but there is no latest-run edge evidence to tune them inside the leader config.
broken_edges.problem: Dead or dormant active-router profiles have no observed edge in the current run.

market_regime_read: The current sample is a favorable long-only continuation/pullback regime with 108 completed trades, 100% realized win rate, $6830.3 total PnL, 0.4% drawdown, and zero slow >=12h trades. The risk-adjusted leader remains run_001/run_005/run_014 at $7125.8 and 107 trades; run_016 added one trade but lost PnL efficiency. The main hidden risk is not realized losses but deep intratrade heat and delayed recovery: worst MAE -527, average MAE about -100, only 9.3% of trades see MFE before MAE, and only 0.9% touch TP before MAE. This calls for new-regime discovery around profile splits and state routing, not broad risk filters that would remove protected fast winners.

new_strategy_ideas.edge: Separate high-extension charm-pin recoveries from normal fast charm-pin pullbacks.
new_strategy_ideas.failure_mode: A blunt config gate blocks profitable extended-VWAP winners; this needs profile-aware state, not a single global threshold.
new_strategy_ideas.name: Charm Pin High-Extension Recovery Classifier
new_strategy_ideas.required_data: Entry-time profile, ES-VWAP distance, VWAP acceleration, rolling-high distance, pullback-from-high, RSI slope, ADX, first-profit delay labels, and protected extended-VWAP winner set.
new_strategy_ideas.trigger: charm_pin_pullback_long with ES-VWAP extension above normal, negative or weak VWAP acceleration, large pullback/rolling-high distance, and no early positive path behavior.
new_strategy_ideas.validation_metric: Reduce charm_pin_pullback_long worst MAE from -527 toward <= -403 while retaining >=90% of charm-pin PnL and >=95% of fast charm-pin winners.

new_strategy_ideas.edge: Route 1DTE pullback-like states away from breakout-only logic when 0DTE is unavailable.
new_strategy_ideas.failure_mode: May cannibalize profitable breakout_long_1dte trades or be unreachable depending on router ordering.
new_strategy_ideas.name: 1DTE Pullback Companion Profile
new_strategy_ideas.required_data: VALID_1DTE_ONLY trades split by ES reclaim/pullback state, structure distances, RSI bucket, ADX slope, and option-strike-map state.
new_strategy_ideas.trigger: VALID_1DTE_ONLY with ES state resembling reclaim/pullback rather than momentum breakout.
new_strategy_ideas.validation_metric: Combined 1DTE PnL >= current $1310, 1DTE average MAE improves from about -124, and total score remains within 2% of leader.

new_strategy_ideas.edge: Discover whether positive charm/delta breakout is a separate valid continuation regime.
new_strategy_ideas.failure_mode: Dormant profile activation could add noisy continuation entries or steal fills from pullback/squeeze profiles.
new_strategy_ideas.name: Delta Charm Breakout Dormant Activation Probe
new_strategy_ideas.required_data: Router activation for inactive delta_charm_breakout_long, plus profile-level trade counts, PnL, MAE, exposure, and overlap with squeeze/pullback entries.
new_strategy_ideas.trigger: VALID_0DTE positive flow/positive charm continuation where current pullback/squeeze profiles do not already claim the entry.
new_strategy_ideas.validation_metric: At least 5 independent trades, positive PnL, PnL/exposure hour >150, worst MAE better than -250, and no degradation of pullback_long or squeeze_long PnL.

new_strategy_ideas.edge: Test whether mean-reversion entries can capture pin/reclaim states currently blocked or routed to tail-heavy pullbacks.
new_strategy_ideas.failure_mode: Mean-reversion may be unavailable, too broad, or conflict with the long-only continuation regime.
new_strategy_ideas.name: Mean Reversion Long Holdout Activation
new_strategy_ideas.required_data: Router activation for inactive mean_reversion_long, profile trade stats, option data state, distance to VWAP/walls, and comparison against pullback_long tail rows.
new_strategy_ideas.trigger: VALID_0DTE pullback/reclaim conditions with weaker continuation signals but positive support/reversion context.
new_strategy_ideas.validation_metric: Positive standalone PnL over >=5 trades, average MAE better than pullback_long, and no worsening of global worst MAE or drawdown.

new_strategy_ideas.edge: Use post-entry path speed as a future risk state rather than an entry-only veto.
new_strategy_ideas.failure_mode: Runtime exits previously damaged objective when too blunt, so this needs a learned or stateful classifier rather than hard adverse-point stops.
new_strategy_ideas.name: Delayed First-Profit Heat State
new_strategy_ideas.required_data: Intratrade path features: first +1.5/+2.0 minute, MFE before MAE, first 15m MFE/MAE, profile, TP distance, and option state.
new_strategy_ideas.trigger: Trades failing to make early positive progress while accumulating adverse movement in profiles with high recovery heat.
new_strategy_ideas.validation_metric: Improve worst/average MAE and exposure hours while preserving >=98% net PnL; reject if realized losses appear or score drops materially.

next_experiments.config_bias: Config-only activation of one dormant profile at a time via router replacement plus minimal profile enablement/tuning. Do not combine with existing edge pruning.
next_experiments.goal: Discover whether an inactive positive-flow continuation profile adds independent profitable trades without damaging current pullback/squeeze/charm-pin edges.
next_experiments.hypothesis: delta_charm_breakout_long may express a cleaner continuation state than call_wall_acceptance_long, whose expansion attempts failed.
next_experiments.name: Dormant Delta-Charm Breakout Probe
next_experiments.primary_metric: Standalone profile PnL, PnL/exposure hour, worst MAE, and total risk-adjusted score versus leader.
next_experiments.risk: Low sample or router cannibalization may make results ambiguous; reject if total PnL falls >2% or pullback/squeeze PnL is displaced.

next_experiments.config_bias: Create a dedicated 1DTE companion test rather than tightening breakout_long_1dte. Keep existing breakout_long_1dte protected and compare combined 1DTE state quality.
next_experiments.goal: Find a 1DTE profile split that reduces MAE without sacrificing the profitable 1DTE fallback edge.
next_experiments.hypothesis: Some VALID_1DTE_ONLY trades are pullback/reclaim states mis-modeled as breakouts; a companion profile may improve average MAE while retaining PnL.
next_experiments.name: 1DTE Pullback Companion Discovery
next_experiments.primary_metric: Combined 1DTE PnL retention >=100% of current, average MAE improvement, total score, and no increase in worst MAE.
next_experiments.risk: Prior 1DTE tightening hurt; profile activation must not remove profitable breakout fallback trades.

next_experiments.config_bias: Study charm_pin_pullback_long as two states: normal fast pullback versus high-extension slow-recovery. Prefer narrow state split or veto-boundary research; avoid global ES-VWAP caps because protected extended-VWAP winners exist.
next_experiments.goal: Reduce the -527 charm-pin tail while retaining the high-frequency fast scalp contribution.
next_experiments.hypothesis: The worst charm-pin tail is a high-extension, negative-acceleration, large pullback/rolling-high state, distinct from the protected fast charm-pin winners.
next_experiments.name: Charm Pin Extension-State Split
next_experiments.primary_metric: charm_pin_pullback_long worst MAE, retained charm-pin PnL >=90%, fast_under_15m trades, and total score.
next_experiments.risk: Extended VWAP is also an edge; overfitting the worst row can block clean extended-VWAP winners.

next_experiments.config_bias: Evaluate mean_reversion_long as a separate activation holdout, not as a replacement for pullback_long. Require independent positive sample and strict no-cannibalization checks.
next_experiments.goal: Discover whether a reversion profile can absorb weak-continuation pullbacks that currently create heat.
next_experiments.hypothesis: Some long pullback tails are reversion-like and may perform better under a profile designed for support/reclaim rather than continuation.
next_experiments.name: Dormant Mean-Reversion Holdout
next_experiments.primary_metric: Standalone PnL, average MAE versus pullback_long, global worst MAE, and pullback_long PnL displacement.
next_experiments.risk: The current regime is continuation-friendly; mean reversion can be redundant or harmful if activated broadly.

next_experiments.config_bias: Freeze call_wall_acceptance_long expansion and instead run an attribution-only or extremely isolated activation diagnostic if required. Do not loosen DEX/call-volume thresholds in the leader path.
next_experiments.goal: Determine whether call-wall acceptance is truly unreachable or simply dominated by router ordering/profile overlap.
next_experiments.hypothesis: The profile has concept appeal but current config and prior expansions show no robust edge; the next useful work is routing attribution, not threshold loosening.
next_experiments.name: Call Wall Reachability Audit
next_experiments.primary_metric: Eligible-but-not-routed count, displaced profile PnL, and hypothetical standalone sample quality.
next_experiments.risk: Further threshold relaxation has repeatedly worsened objective and MAE.

next_experiments.config_bias: Use only narrow, profile-aware tail-state tests. Avoid broad ADX-slope >=0, setup floor, structure-score raise, or entry-veto-point escalation because counterfactuals block large protected PnL.
next_experiments.goal: Improve risk-adjusted score by reducing deep heat without sacrificing the fast winner cohort.
next_experiments.hypothesis: Bad-MAE rows are better explained by profile/state interaction than by any single universal entry threshold.
next_experiments.name: Protected-Winner Constraint Framework
next_experiments.primary_metric: Worst MAE, average MAE, retained protected fast-winner PnL, total net profit >=98% of leader, and score.
next_experiments.risk: Without protected-winner constraints, optimizer may choose deceptively clean filters that destroy core edge.

research_notes: Leader evidence is stable: run_001/run_005/run_014 remain best with identical score and $7125.8 PnL. Recent run_016 is close but not better, and its charm-wall structure-gate test lost score. The optimizer should prioritize discovery runs that add or split regimes rather than refine already-failed broad gates. The central constraint is protecting 58 fast winners and 18 extended-VWAP winners; they explain why simple ADX slope, setup floor, structure score, or VWAP-distance rules look attractive on MAE but are likely destructive. Dormant profile tests are acceptable only as isolated router activation experiments with explicit cannibalization checks.
risk_controls: Reject any experiment that reduces total net profit below 98% of the leader unless worst MAE improves dramatically and average MAE improves materially.
Reject broad entry filters that block more than 10% of protected fast-winner PnL without a clear worst-MAE improvement below -403.
Keep breakout_long_1dte active; prior 1DTE tightening was harmful, so only companion/split tests are justified.
Do not combine dormant activation with tail-veto edits in the same run; attribution must remain clean.
Require per-profile PnL displacement reporting for any router activation, especially pullback_long, squeeze_long, and breakout_long_1dte.
Treat no-0DTE fallback changes as holdout-only because sample size is 6 trades and prior pruning did not improve objective.
Do not promote call-wall threshold loosening unless it independently clears sample, PnL, MAE, and no-cannibalization hurdles.
